notes: Professor list reflects courses scheduled in GoSOLAR — courses with no scheduled sections have empty professors arrays.
Run phase1_gosolar_allterms.js across all 12 semesters and re-merge to get a complete professor list.
RMP fields are null until phase2_ratemyprof.js is run.
CSC 3780 (GoSOLAR) was mapped to CSC 4780 (catalog) — same course, different legacy code.

courses.title: Computers and Applications
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: high school Algebra II.
courses.description: A tech literacy course for non-CS majors — covers how computers and the internet work, common software tools, and the basics of digital security with no programming required.

courses.title: Principles of Computer Science I
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Only for STEM majors and minors. Co-requisite: CSC 1301L. Students must be enrolled in MATH 1111 or 1113 (or a higher level mathematics course excluding MATH 1401) or have previously completed one of these courses with a C or higher.
courses.description: Your first real coding class for STEM majors — you'll learn Python from scratch, writing programs that use variables, loops, functions, and basic algorithms to solve real problems.

courses.title: Principles of Computer Science I
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Only for majors in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics or Engineering, or minor in Computer Science. Students must be enrolled in MATH 1111 or 1113 (or a higher level mathematics course excluding MATH 1401) or have previously completed one of these courses with a C or higher.
courses.description: Same intro Python content as CSC 1301 with the lab built into one 4-credit course — designed for CS, math, physics, and engineering majors who want lecture and lab combined.

courses.title: Principles of Computer Science Laboratory I
courses.prerequisites: None
courses.description: Weekly coding lab that runs alongside CSC 1301 — you put lecture concepts into practice by writing and submitting Python programs in a hands-on setting.

courses.title: Principles of Computer Science II
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 1301 and CSC 1301L with a C or higher. Corequisite: CSC 1302L.
courses.description: Learning more about Python and diving into object-oriented programming you'll design classes, use inheritance and interfaces, and build more complex programs using fundamental data structures.

courses.title: Principles of Computer Science II
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 1301K with a C or higher, or CSC 1301 and CSC 1301L, each with a C or higher.
courses.description: Same OOP and Python content as CSC 1302 with the lab integrated into one course — for students in the K-track who want lecture and lab combined.

courses.title: Principles of Computer Science Laboratory II
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Only for majors in STEM majors or minors. Co-requisite: CSC 1302.
courses.description: Hands-on lab companion to CSC 1302 where you implement the Python and OOP concepts from lecture through graded programming exercises.

courses.title: Introduction to Information Security and Privacy
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 1302 with a C or better.
courses.description: A practical first look at cybersecurity — you'll get hands-on exposure to encryption, network attacks, access control models, and privacy tools, building a broad foundation before you specialize.

courses.title: Theoretical Foundations of Computer Science
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Math 1113 or Math 1220 with a C or higher.
courses.description: The math CS majors actually need covers logic, proofs by induction, set theory, combinatorics, and graph theory, all taught through the lens of how they underpin algorithms, databases, and program correctness.

courses.title: Data Structures
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 1302 and (CSC 2510 or MATH 2420) with a C or higher; and MATH 2211 with a C or higher or concurrent enrollment.
courses.description: Where you stop just writing code and start thinking about efficiency — you'll implement and analyze linked lists, trees, hash tables, and sorting algorithms, and learn to reason about time and space complexity.

courses.title: Ethical and Social Issues in Computing
courses.prerequisites: MATH 1113
courses.description: A writing-heavy course examining the real-world impact of technology on society — covers AI bias, surveillance, privacy, intellectual property, and what ethical responsibility looks like for software engineers.

courses.title: Computer Organization and Programming
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 1302, and CSc 2510 or Math 2420 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: You'll see what's actually happening under your Python or Java code — write x86 assembly programs and understand how the CPU executes instructions, manages memory, and handles the call stack.

courses.title: System-Level Programming
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 1302, CSC 1302L, and either CSC 2510 or MATH 2420 with a of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement or be enrolled in an approved computing program to enroll in this course.
courses.description: A C programming course focused on talking directly to the OS — you'll write programs that open files, spawn processes, use pipes, and make Linux system calls, the way real systems software is built.

courses.title: Software Development-CTW
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 2720 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: A software design course where you learn to plan before you code — covers UML modeling, design patterns, and test-driven development; writing is a significant graded component (CTW designation).

courses.title: Technical Interview Preparation I
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 2720 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Structured prep for technical job interviews run in partnership with industry — expect algorithmic problem solving, mock interviews, resume review, and soft-skills coaching.

courses.title: Introduction to Embedded Systems Laboratory
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: A hands-on course where you program microcontrollers and embedded hardware to respond to the physical world — bridges the gap between software and the hardware it runs on.

courses.title: Introduction to Robotics
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSc 3210 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: You build and program physical robots using a hardware kit — teaches how to translate software logic into real-world movement in a way that's more hands-on than most CS courses.

courses.title: Computer Architecture
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3210 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Goes deeper into how CPUs are actually designed — covers logic gates, combinational and sequential circuits, memory hierarchies, pipelining, and how instruction sets get implemented in hardware.

courses.title: Computer Networks
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 and either Math 3020 or Math 3030 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: How the internet works, layer by layer — you'll study the full TCP/IP protocol stack from physical transmission up through application-level protocols like HTTP, DNS, and routing algorithms.

courses.title: Mobile Computing and Wireless Network Security
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 and CSc 3320 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: How wireless networks are designed and secured — covers the protocols behind WiFi, cellular, and sensor networks, and the unique security challenges that come with communication over the air.

courses.title: Fundamentals of Cybersecurity
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 and CSc 3320 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: A comprehensive look at how systems get attacked and how to defend them — covers encryption, malware, OS hardening, network security, and the legal and ethical frameworks around cybersecurity.

courses.title: Privacy
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 and either Math 3020 or Math 3030 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: An upper-level course on formally defining and enforcing privacy — covers differential privacy, anonymization techniques, and where privacy law intersects with technical implementation.

courses.title: Ethical Hacking
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSc 2720 (Data Structures) and CSc 3320 (System Level Programming). Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Teaches the mindset and techniques of penetration testers — you'll learn how attackers find and exploit vulnerabilities so you can identify and fix them before someone else does.

courses.title: Secure Software Engineering
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSc 2720 and CSc 3320 with a C or better. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: How to write software that doesn't get hacked — covers secure coding practices, common vulnerability classes (buffer overflows, injection attacks), static analysis tools, and how to bake security into the development lifecycle.

courses.title: Network Security
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 4220 with a C or higher or equivalent. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Deep dive into the protocols that secure internet communication — covers TLS/SSL, firewalls, intrusion detection systems, public-key cryptography, and authentication systems like Kerberos.

courses.title: Security in Internet of Things
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSC 4220 with a C or higher or equivalent. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Why securing IoT devices is so hard and what the solutions look like — covers the attack surface of connected devices, lightweight cryptography, and real-world security challenges in smart homes and industrial systems.

courses.title: Malware Analysis and Defense
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSc 3210 Computer Organization and Programming, CSc 2720 Data Structure. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: You analyze real malicious software to understand how it works — covers static and dynamic malware analysis, reverse engineering techniques, and building defenses against common malware families.

courses.title: Computer Forensics
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSc 2720 (Data Structures) and CSc 3320 (System Level Programming). Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: How digital investigations work in practice — covers collecting and preserving evidence from computers and networks, file system forensics, disk imaging, and how findings get used in legal proceedings.

courses.title: Digital Image Processing
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 or DSCI 2720 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: The algorithms that let computers understand and manipulate images — covers filtering, edge detection, transforms, compression, and segmentation, with heavy use of the underlying linear algebra.

courses.title: Parallel and Distributed Computing
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSc 3210 and CSc 3320 with grades of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: How to write software that runs across multiple processors or machines simultaneously — covers threading models, MPI, distributed algorithms, and the coordination and consistency problems that arise.

courses.title: Cloud Computing
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 2720 or DSCI 2720 with C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: How cloud infrastructure works and how to build on it — covers virtualization, containers, IaaS/PaaS/SaaS models, and how to deploy and scale applications on platforms like AWS or Azure.

courses.title: Operating Systems
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: What happens inside the OS while your program runs — covers process scheduling, memory management, file systems, and concurrency, with programming assignments that interact with the Linux kernel.

courses.title: Programming Language Concepts
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720, CSc 3210, and CSc 3320 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: A theoretical lens on programming languages — you'll study how different languages handle scoping, type systems, and memory management, and understand why languages are designed the way they are.

courses.title: Compilers
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 and CSc 3210 with grades of “C” or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: How a compiler turns source code into machine instructions — you'll build key components of one, working through lexical analysis, parsing, semantic checking, and code generation.

courses.title: Software Engineering - CTW
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720, CSc 3210, and CSc 3320 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: A senior-level software engineering course where you apply the full development lifecycle — requirements, design, implementation, and testing — on a large project, with significant technical writing required.

courses.title: Capstone Senior Design I-CTW
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 3350 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: First half of the senior capstone — your team picks a project, writes a formal specification, designs the architecture, and produces a development plan ready for implementation.

courses.title: Capstone Senior Design II-CTW
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 4351 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Second half of the capstone — you build, test, and present the system your team designed in CSC 4351, with faculty or industry mentorship throughout.

courses.title: Mobile Application Development
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 with a grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course. DescriptionCrosslisted with CSC 6360. Crosslisted with CSc 6360. This course will cover the technologies, tools, frameworks and languages that are most commonly used in developing mobile applications for multiple mobile platforms. Topics include mobile application design, user interfaces, mobile application demographic and platform delivery, mobile networking, hosting infrastructure, and mobile security.
courses.description: Build real apps for iOS and Android — covers the dominant mobile frameworks, designing for small screens and touch input, handling offline behavior, and getting an app shipped to an app store.

courses.title: Web Programming
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Full-stack web development the old-school way — you'll build server-side apps using Java Servlets, JSP, and PHP, wire them to MySQL databases, and work with HTML forms and XML.

courses.title: Automata
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: The theoretical limits of computation — you'll prove what problems can and can't be solved by machines, using finite automata, context-free grammars, pushdown automata, and Turing machines.

courses.title: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: (CSc 2720 or DSCI 2720) and either Math 3020 or Math 3030 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: The core algorithms course — you'll prove correctness and analyze time/space complexity for divide-and-conquer, greedy, dynamic programming, and graph algorithms, and touch on NP-completeness.

courses.title: Numerical Analysis I
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: Math 2215 with grade of C or higher and the ability to program in a high-level language. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Nature of error; iteration; techniques for nonlinear systems; zeros of functions; interpolation; numerical differentiation; Newton-Cotes formulae for definite integrals; computer implementation of algorithms.

courses.title: Numerical Analysis II
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: Either Math 3030 or both Math 2641 and Math 2215 with grades of C or higher, and the ability to program in a high level language. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Gaussian Elimination for linear systems; least squares; Taylor, predictor-corrector and Runge- Kutta methods for solving ordinary differential equations; boundary value problems; partial differential equations.

courses.title: Matlab
courses.prerequisites: None

courses.title: Introduction to Bioinformatics
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720, Biol 1103K, [or Biol 1103 and Biol 1103L] and Chem 1211K [or Chem 1211 and Chem 1211L] with grades of “C” or higher.Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: The explosion in biological knowledge due to various genome projects and other high-throughput techniques has created entirely new fields and industries, and a need for trained computational biologists who are familiar with Biology, Statistics, and Computer Science.

courses.title: Database Systems
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CS 2720 with grade of “C” or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: An introduction to the fundamental concepts and principles that underlie the relational model of data. Topics include formal query languages; SQL; query optimization; relational database design theory; physical database design, integrity, security, and concurrency control.

courses.title: Human-Computer Interaction
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Techniques and methodologies for development of user interfaces in software systems; topics include interaction styles, interaction devices, user documentation, and interface assessment.

courses.title: Data Visualization
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Data visualization or displaying data in visual forms and is closely related to data analytics. In this class, students will study the theories of data visualization, design principles, and data visualization techniques.

courses.title: Data Mining
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Introduction to basic data mining techniques (such as association rules mining, cluster analysis, and classification methods) and their applications (such as Web data mining, biomedical data mining and security).

courses.title: Semantic Web
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 or DSCI 2720 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: In-depth overview of the Semantic Web and how it can be applied. Major topics include core technical components and language constructs for the Semantic Web, linked data concepts/projects and RDF triple stores, and real world semantic Web applications.

courses.title: Big Data Programming
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course. DescriptionCrosslisted with CSC 6760. Crosslisted with CSc 6760. This course will cover the technologies, tools, frameworks and languages that are most commonly used in Big Data Programming. Focus will be on algorithms for analyzing and mining massive datasets, graphs and social network data. Topics include the storage, management, processing and analysis of massive datasets, as well as Big Data governance, security, and privacy issues.
courses.description: This course will cover the technologies, tools, frameworks and languages that are most commonly used in Big Data Programming.

courses.title: Fundamentals of Data Science
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 2720 with a C or better. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Introduction to the fundamental concepts of predictive data science for tabular data with qualitative and quantitative scales.

courses.title: Artificial Intelligence
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSc 2720 or DSCI 2720 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: An overview of techniques and methodologies in the field of artificial intelligence. Topics may include search strategies, problem solving, natural language processing, logic and deduction, memory models, learning, expert systems, knowledge representation, and robotics.

courses.title: Interactive Computer Graphics
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 3320 with grade of “C” or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: This course will introduce students to 3D computer graphics and game programming. Students will learn how to develop 3D games and interactive computer graphics applications (such as virtual reality) using game engines.

courses.title: Fundamentals of Game Design
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Covers major aspects of game design such as challenges, gameplay, actions, core mechanics, worlds, characters, game balancing, user interfaces, and game genres.

courses.title: Mobile Game Development
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 3320 with grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: This course focuses on developing games for mobile platforms. Topics include mobile platforms, mobile game design principles, game engine, graphics, game physics, audio, mobile game input, playtesting, and publishing.

courses.title: Extended Reality Engineering
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: CSC3320 or CSC3350 with grade of C or higher.
courses.description: This course offers an overview of extended reality (XR) engineering characteristics and introduces the development approaches, focusing on the life cycle and key activities at each stage of XR development.

courses.title: Advanced Computer Graphics Programming
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with a grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Students will learn advanced 3D graphics and game programming. The topics will include rendering, lighting, camera, animation, user interaction, physics simulation, game AI, and GPU.

courses.title: Computer Animation
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSc 3320 with a grade of C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: The basics of three-dimensional computer animation including 3D modeling, lighting, texture mapping, key framing, character animation, rigid and soft body dynamics, particles, cloth, hair, fluid, etc.

courses.title: Machine Learning
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 2720 and Math 2641 with a grade of C or better. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: This course is intended to provide a general introduction to machine learning. This course will cover the fundamental concepts and principles of supervised learning, unsupervised learning, semi-supervised learning and reinforcement learning.

courses.title: Introduction to Deep Learning
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 2720 (or DSCI 2720) and MATH 2641 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic concepts and algorithms of deep neural networks and its applications to computer vision and natural language processing.

courses.title: Honors Thesis I
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Good standing with the Honors College and consent of instructor. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Readings or research preparatory to honors thesis or project. Signature Experience course.

courses.title: Honors Thesis II
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Good standing with the Honors College and consent of instructor.Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Writing or production of honors thesis or project. Signature Experience course.

courses.title: Technical Interview Preparation II
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: CSC 2720 with a C or higher. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: The course prepares students for careers in Computer Science through a collaborative program between the CSC department and strategic partners.

courses.title: Computer Science Internship
courses.prerequisites: None

courses.title: Topics in Computer Science
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: Consent of Instructor. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Selected topics in Computer Science will be covered. Topics include the latest advances in computing.

courses.title: Undergraduate Research in Computer Science
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: at least 12 upper-division hours in computer science. Authorization required. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Independent investigation of topics of common interest to student and instructor. This course may include a Signature Experience component.

courses.title: Interdisciplinary Topics
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisites: Consent of instructor. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Selected Interdisciplinary topics. May be repeated if topics are different.

courses.title: Directed Readings BIS-CTW
courses.prerequisites: None

courses.title: Student Research and Teaching Support
courses.prerequisites: None

courses.title: Selected Topics
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: consent of instructor. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: No more than six credit hours may be applied toward the major. May be repeated if topics are different.

courses.title: Directed Readings
courses.prerequisites: Prerequisite: GPA of 3.0 or higher and consent of instructor. Students must meet the Computer Science Major Eligibility Requirement in order to enroll in this course.
courses.description: Directed study with a faculty member.
